content.problemStatement:
Chef recently realized that he needs a haircut, and went to his favorite hair salon. At the salon, he found NNN customers waiting for their haircuts. From his past experience, Chef knows that the salon takes MMM minutes per customer. Only one person can get their haircut at a time.

For how many minutes will Chef have to wait before he can get his haircut?


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM, as described in the problem.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of minutes that Chef will have to wait before he can get his haircut.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and M, where N is the number of customers already waiting and M is the time (in minutes) the salon needs for one haircut.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with one integer – the total minutes Chef has to wait before his turn, i.e., N × M.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 2 customers each need 15 minutes → 2×15 = 30 minutes.\nTest case 2: 0 customers → 0×15 = 0 minutes.
Each line multiplies N by M: 5×10=50, 1×100=100, 1000×1=1000.

solution.approach: For each test case the waiting time is simply the product of the number of customers already in line (N) and the time per haircut (M). No additional data structures are needed. Read the input, compute N*M using 64‑bit integer arithmetic to avoid overflow, and output the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time multiplication per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of input size.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(data[idx]); m = int(data[idx+1]); idx += 2
        out.append(str(n * m))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
